params.name: configKey
params.desc: REST client configuration key identifying the DocuWare connection from rest-clients.yaml

params.name: documentId
params.desc: The unique identifier of the document to delete

params.name: fileCabinetId
params.desc: The unique identifier of the file cabinet containing the document

name: **Description
This API endpoint is used to delete a specific document from a file cabinet in the specified platform. The document is identified by its unique document ID, which is provided in the URL path.
**Integration
deleteDocument(String, String, String)
- Deletes a document from a DocuWare file cabinet.
- Calls REST endpoint /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Documents/{DocumentId} via the delete RestClientCall in DeleteService.p.json.
- Maps FileCabinetId and DocumentId into the request path from input parameters.
- Sends configKey as REST client configuration via properties.configKey.
- Uses DocuWareService.get().throwErrorIfUnsuccessful(response); in the response entity script to throw an error for unsuccessful responses.
- Returns no payload; success is indicated by a successful HTTP response.
Parameters:
- configKey (String): REST client configuration key to identify the DocuWare server connection from rest-clients.yaml
- fileCabinetId (String): The unique identifier of the file cabinet containing the document
- documentId (String): The unique identifier of the document to move
- Default / Return: Returns void (no return object); the operation completes successfully if DocuWare responds without error.
**delete
Call REST Client APIs from Docuware.
**How to integrate
- Add Call: Add a Call element to your process that invokes the DeleteService sub-process using signature deleteDocument(String, String, String).
- Prepare data: Populate the parameters before calling:
+ configKey: REST client configuration key matching the entry in config/rest-clients.yaml
+ documentId: ID of the document to delete
+ fileCabinetId: ID of the file cabinet containing the document
- Call REST API: DeleteService.p.json sends the request to:
+ DELETE /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Documents/{DocumentId}
- Endpoint mapping: The delete RestClientCall maps FileCabinetId and DocumentId into the path template and sends configKey as properties.configKey.
- Response handling: Uses DocuWareService.get().throwErrorIfUnsuccessful(response); in the response entity script to raise an error for any unsuccessful response.
- Return object: This operation does not return a payload; success is confirmed by a successful HTTP response and no thrown exception.
- Post-success behavior: After success, the specified document is deleted from the targeted DocuWare file cabinet.
- Example (conceptual):
+ Set variables:
configKey = "02d1eec1-32e9-4316-afc3-793448486203"
documentId = "DOC-456"
fileCabinetId = "FC-123"
+ Call the DeleteService sub-process with the matching signature
+ After the Call returns, the document has been deleted if no error occurred
- Notes & tips:
+ configKey must match the RestClients entry so URL and credentials are correct
+ documentId must refer to an existing document in the specified file cabinet
+ This operation does not upload or transfer files; it deletes an existing document
+ Use the absence of an exception and a successful HTTP response to verify completion

Summary: Add a Call to DeleteService with deleteDocument(String, String, String), set configKey, documentId, and fileCabinetId, call DELETE /FileCabinets/{FileCabinetId}/Documents/{DocumentId}, and confirm success by the successful response.
